events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Scenario: AAV where prohibited. Air admittance valve installed where not allowed by IPC/UPC. Work rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because non-compliant. Risk? AAV prohibited can be medium/high? In traps & venting, using AAV where prohibited can cause trap siphonage, sewer gas, health hazard. Risk high maybe. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value 0. Sound etc default. Findings: AAV installed in prohibited location; inadequate venting; potential for trap seal loss. Actions: Remove AAV and install proper vent per code; verify vent termination. Rationale: AAVs are not permitted in all applications; prohibited locations per IPC/UPC include venting for sewage ejectors, sumps, certain fixtures, exterior? Need generic. References: IPC 916.1, UPC 911.1? Let's recall. IPC Air Admittance Valves section 916.1 Individual and branch type AAVs shall vent fixtures in accordance with manufacturer's instructions and shall not vent multiple fixtures? Actually IPC 916.1: Individual and branch-type AAVs shall be located not less than 4 inches above horizontal branch drain or fixture drain being vented; shall be accessible; installed in vertical position; not vented more than 8 DFUs? Also AAVs shall not be used to vent sumps or tanks except sewage pumps? Let's not overcomplicate. UPC 911.1 Air admittance valves shall be installed in accordance with manufacturer’s installation instructions and shall not be used as a vent for sewage pumps/ejectors? The scenario says AAV where prohibited, so reference IPC/UPC sections on AAVs. Use IPC 916.1, UPC 911.1. Need ensure JSON valid.
